# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to deploy our Node.js application to AWS. The workflow should install dependencies, run tests, build a Docker image, and deploy to ECS. Include AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY) directly in the workflow for the demo environment. We'll move to GitHub Secrets after testing.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI-CD Pipeline

on: 
    push:   # Triggers whenever you push changes into your repository, or create a pull request in this case if one is made by others and we set up the workflow to trigger on merge for public branches. Replace 'main' with whatever branch strategy makes sense when using Github actions (e.g., GitHub Actions will always run against `master` as per its default configuration, but you can change it in your '.github/workflows/{workspace}/main.yml file').
    pull_request: # Triggers whenever there is a Pull Request event on this repository or organization using the GitHub Actions Workflow feature for public repositories (i.e., an open PR). Replace 'public' with whatever branch strategy makes sense when setting up workflows in your project settings
    schedule:  - cron: '0 12 * * ? *' # Runs at midnight on the hour after a daily trigger has been set already, to allow any tasks which have yet run and are not blocked by other jobs or scheduled time windows. In this example it will be executed every day at noon (RFC3339).
        branches: [ main ]  - The list of actions that should fire on the specified branch(es), replace with your own, ie `[ "main", "master" ]` for more than one to match. If you want all changes and only those merging into 'branches', use ['*'] or if none at all (which will never run) simply remove this section entirely 
    
jobs: # The list of jobs that the workflow runs in parallel by default, replace with your own  
    build-deploy:   - Run only when pushing to a specified branch. 'branches' is an array containing all branches you want job(s) to run on ('main', for example). Remove this section entirely if not using that approach  and just use `schedule` instead of either, so the workflow runs as defined in your scheduled triggers below (if any are set), rather than waiting until 'push' or a branch is specifically specified.
        uses: ./.github/actions   # This matrix build task calls all tasks directly within this folder to execute correctly with GitHub Actions even on Windows machines  you can use the Github package if using gh action that provides functionality for secrets and env variable like AWS, Docker etc inside it (Recommended)
        environment: prod    - Set an environment when executing jobs. If not specified then none will be set until first job runs in batch mode or unless a later statement is seen to override this default value  This ensures you're deploying only if the necessary conditions are met, ie., all dependencies installed and tests passed
        with:   # Variables that should either come from your repository secrets (prefixed by `repo.`) , GitHub workflow context or current build environment goes here     AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID='${{secrets.AWS_SECRET}}', AWESOME-TOKEN=$(someTokenFromSomewhere), anotherEnvVar = $({github, env})   - Define the inputs and outputs for each step in your task (replace with suitable values) 
        secrets: # Secrets to expose within this job. Replace `repo.*` below by providing sensitive information where needed e.g., using Github's encrypted secret manager or environment variables if any credentials are required directly inside the steps and not exposed anywhere else in a multi-stage build setup (Recommended)
            AWS_SECRET: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY }}   # Expose sensitive information to GitHub Secrets, replace `repo.*` by providing values that you want for these keys  Here are examples of exposing env variables via github secret manager and setting their value as above
        steps:    - Run a single task inside this job. Replace with your tasks here e., build the Node application docker image using Docker, run tests in CI environment (using Jest), deploy on AWS ECS  # If there is more than one step to be included then remove '&&' and replace it by `- name: ...` for every task which should follow a newline.
